verification_plan.method_assignment_rationale: Quantitative constraint can be planned against approved source and quantitative packet without execution.
verification_plan.criteria_seed: Confirm rail.vout requirement is 3.3 V within +/-5% using approved authority values.

verification_plan.method_assignment_rationale: Harness status is review_required and owner decision is missing before inspection or test planning can mature.

verification_plan.method_assignment_rationale: Claim explicitly requires simulation, but model, deck, stimuli, and measurement criteria must exist first.

verification_plan.claim: Configuration baseline consistency for board revision
verification_plan.method_assignment_rationale: Trace/configuration conflict must be resolved by inspecting approved baseline authority before affected tests can be planned.

missing official source ref and owner intent

method_map.rationale: Use for quantitative requirement planning where approved numeric authority is present and conflict-free.
Use where owner intent, review disposition, or source authority is missing.
Use for startup transient planning only after required simulation prerequisites are supplied.
Use for source and configuration baseline consistency review.

evidence_need_register.needed_evidence: Approved source value and quantitative packet value for rail.vout tolerance calculation.
Owner decision on J1.1_to_U1.VIN harness status.
Test fixture procedure for harness connection verification.
Simulation model, deck, stimuli, and measurement criteria.
Official source reference for status LED function claim.
Owner intent decision for regulator-enabled LED behavior.
Resolved configuration baseline selecting board rev A or rev B.

verification_gap_register.impact: Blocks mature inspection/test planning for VIN harness claim.

verification_gap_register.gap: Simulation model, deck, stimuli, and measurement criteria missing.
verification_gap_register.impact: Simulation can be planned only as prerequisite-gated.
Function claim cannot receive criteria seed.

verification_gap_register.gap: Trace says board rev A while configuration packet says rev B.
verification_gap_register.impact: Affected test readiness is blocked by source conflict.

test_or_simulation_readiness.note: Planning criteria can be seeded from approved quantitative evidence.
Owner disposition and fixture procedure are prerequisites.
Simulation is not execution-ready until model, deck, stimuli, and measurements are defined.
Official source and owner intent are absent.
Board revision conflict blocks affected readiness.

owner_followup_needed.request: Decide whether J1.1_to_U1.VIN harness claim is approved, rejected, or requires redesign.
Provide or authorize a test fixture procedure for harness verification planning.
Provide simulation model, deck, stimuli, and measurement criteria.
Provide official source reference and owner intent for status LED behavior.
Resolve board revision baseline conflict between rev A trace and rev B configuration packet.

trr_readiness_handoff.trr_note: Candidate for TRR package inclusion as planning-only analysis item.
Hold for owner harness decision and fixture procedure.
Hold simulation readiness until model/deck/stimuli/measurements exist.
Exclude from TRR readiness until source and owner intent are supplied.
Exclude affected tests until board revision conflict is resolved.

fca_svr_handoff_index.handoff_use: Open harness decision and fixture procedure dependency.
Unsubstantiated function claim requiring source and intent.
Configuration baseline conflict requiring resolution before conformance review.

verification_plan_provenance.source_limit: Fixture-only; no file reads, commands, source acquisition, or execution performed.

verification_plan_summary.summary: Planning records were created for all fixture traces with stable item ids, method rationale, evidence needs, gaps, and TRR/FCA-SVR handoff notes.

boundary_review_note.note: This output is a synthetic public-safe planning artifact only. It does not claim verification execution, pass/fail, approval, source acquisition, simulation execution, physical test execution, or acceptance.
